reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js runs the REAL transformers.js text-to-speech pipeline on `Xenova/mms-tts-eng`: `pipeline('text-to-speech', modelId, { dtype:'q8', device:'wasm' })` then `tts(text)` — Meta's MMS-TTS is an end-to-end VITS model (conditional VAE + normalizing-flow decoder + STOCHASTIC duration predictor, no separate vocoder). out.audio (Float32Array) + out.sampling_rate are used directly. Conformance calls-real-inference (source pattern includes pipeline( and createModelLoader) + io-shape-semantic (playable audio whose speech matches the input text) cover it. Code-grounded backfill; the page measures + reports real gen time/rtf.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back. Because the duration predictor is stochastic, re-running the same text yields a different waveform — the page tracks that real run-to-run duration spread.

rubric.evidence: Input = text. Output = a mono Float32Array waveform at sampling_rate 16000 (single-stage VITS — no spectrogram→vocoder hop). The page encodes it to a 16-bit WAV for <audio> + Download .wav and draws the same samples as the waveform. The see-inside surface reports samples/rate/duration + the number of runs of this text and the ±ms duration spread across runs (the stochastic timing).
rubric.notes: The English checkpoint of a family covering 1000+ languages (one checkpoint = one language).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mms-tts-eng, linked; ONNX of facebook/mms-tts-eng), the task (text-to-speech, VitsModel), the VITS architecture, the output shape (mono Float32Array @ 16000), quantisation (q8), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), download (~38 MB per language), and license (CC-BY-NC 4.0, non-commercial, stated in at-a-glance AND the footer). The worker keeps a per-modelId pipeline cache so the multilingual page can switch languages without reloading. Conformance declares-model-id/task/quantisation(q8)/backend covered.
rubric.notes: device is fixed 'wasm' (VITS runs everywhere); the readout reports the real backend.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader ({ modelId:'Xenova/mms-tts-eng', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:38, requiresWebGPU:false }); a valid current cached model auto-initialises with no Load click; onReady enables Speak. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader + auto-init-on-cached cover it.
rubric.notes: Tiny ~38 MB per language — fast to cache; the worker's Map cache avoids re-loading languages already fetched.

rubric.evidence: #status is role=status aria-live=polite. Loading/synthesising/ready are announced; failures surface on the page: the catch block sets 'Synthesis failed: <message>' with .err, and an empty-text guard shows 'Type something to speak.' Download progress flows from the pipeline progress_callback. The shared loader exposes retry/recovery.
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: All controls real + keyboard-operable: the text <textarea> (with a wrapping label), four sample chips (intro/pangram/notification/meta) that set the textarea, the Speak <button> (relabels to 'Speak again' after the first run — supporting the stochastic-timing comparison), native <audio controls>, and the Download .wav link. buttons-have-names passes.
rubric.notes: The relabel-to-'Speak again' pattern is a nice nudge toward the stochastic-prosody demonstration.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the audio, WAV, waveform, and the duration-spread stats are all derived from the real VITS run; re-running genuinely produces a different waveform (real stochastic duration), which the ±ms spread readout reflects. Conformance no-fake-output (manual-evidenced) holds.

Every button has an accessible name; the waveform canvas carries aria-label='Generated speech waveform'; status uses role=status aria-live; the text input has a wrapping <label>; the readout duplicates samples/rate/duration/runs/spread as text. Light+dark WCAG-AA design system. buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass.

.tts-grid + .sample-row flex-wrap, textarea + canvas inline-size:100%, readout wraps. responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile conformance assert no horizontal overflow. models.json support is needs-review with an automated note flagging sub-24px targets (desktop 12, mobile 7).
rubric.notes: HONEST WEAKNESS: this demo has the highest flagged sub-24px count of the batch (12 desktop / 7 mobile) — largely the sample chips at .2rem/.6rem padding + native <audio> controls. Follow-up matrix pass should raise chip tap size to ≈44px and flip support to ok.

rubric.evidence: Synthesis runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction over ~600 canvas columns, redrawn only on play/seek/resize. No main-thread inference or unbounded loop. Conformance inference-off-main-thread passes.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface; the tiny model keeps generation quick.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in web worker; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — VITS synthesis runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel reduction, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .tts-grid/.sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas inline-size:100%; readout wraps. Follow-up: raise the sub-24px chip tap targets (highest count in the batch).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile assert no-horizontal-overflow; support needs-review for tap size

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates, canvas labelling, media element accessibility
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; use native <audio controls>.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — #status is role=status aria-live=polite; sample chips + buttons carry accessible names; native <audio controls>; the waveform canvas is aria-labelled.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas waveform content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give a meaningful accessible name to canvas-rendered content and duplicate its information as text.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the waveform canvas has aria-label='Generated speech waveform'; the numeric readout (samples/rate/duration/runs/spread) conveys the same information as text.

openQuestions: This demo has the highest flagged sub-24px tap-target count in the batch (12 desktop / 7 mobile) — how many are the native <audio> controls versus the sample chips we should enlarge?

The worker caches a pipeline per modelId for language switching, but this page only loads mms-tts-eng — is the multilingual switcher exercised on the practical use-case page, and does it re-init cleanly per language?

followUpGoals.goal: Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, raise sample-chip tap targets to ≈44px (priority given the high flagged count), and flip models.json support from needs-review to ok.
Add a small waveform/duration overlay across multiple runs of the same text to make the stochastic-duration-predictor variation visible at a glance (beyond the ±ms number).

summary: Retrospective code-grounded critique backfill for mms-tts (Xenova/mms-tts-eng, text-to-speech / VitsModel, q8, ~38 MB, CC-BY-NC 4.0, WASM). REAL inference: the worker runs the transformers.js TTS pipeline (end-to-end VITS — no separate vocoder, stochastic duration predictor) off the main thread and transfers the 16 kHz Float32 waveform back; re-running the same text genuinely changes the timing, which the ±ms duration-spread readout reflects. Controls (text, sample chips, Speak/Speak-again, audio, download) are all real; the waveform + stats are the real output. Auto-init via createModelLoader; CC-BY-NC 4.0 in at-a-glance + footer. HONEST WEAKNESS: this demo has the batch's highest flagged sub-24px tap-target count (12 desktop / 7 mobile), support needs-review — a follow-up matrix pass should raise chip tap sizes. guidanceConsulted: performance, css-layout, accessibility, expose-canvas-content-to-browser-features. Grounded in code + the immutable conformance suite; no measured numbers fabricated.
